batch_summary.note: Apr-Jun 2023. Two RF-14 primary flags: S-001 car unattended safety allegation (Apr 22-28) and S-002 injury relay with pattern claim (May 10). Both delivered in hostile context alongside Abby-targeting. ARC-RF14-001 advances to signal count 3. RF-01 Abby-attack pattern onset in this batch (B-007, per RF-01 record).

primary_flags.description: ROW-2373: 'Don't be leaving Olivia in the car by herself. Cars are getting stolen all the time.' Melissa issues safety directive about Eli leaving Olivia unattended in car. ROW-2402-2403 (Apr 28): 'Olivia was crying after you dropped her off. She said that after she said she was...You shouldn't have left her in the car unsupervised.' Follow-up reinforcing the allegation. Delivered in hostile context alongside Abby-targeting (RF-04 compound). Per ADD-007: consistent with manufactured safety allegation pattern.
primary_flags.rf15_compound: RF-15 peripheral — safety allegation in hostile context; part of manufactured safety narrative (ADD-007)

primary_flags.description: ROW-2464: 'For clarity she said you hurt it because you picked her up and her outfit went u[p]...' — Olivia relay about genital area injury from Eli picking her up. ROW-2469: 'Still hurting but not screaming when she pees. You need to be more careful with her.' Pattern claim included. Delivered via Olivia relay. Genuine minor injury possible (clothing-related), but framing implies ongoing harm from Eli ('you hurt it').
primary_flags.rf15_compound: RF-15 Category A peripheral — harm relay allegation via Olivia as messenger

peripheral_flags.note: Harm relay — injury attributed to Eli via Olivia relay. RF-15 primary for relay mechanism; RF-14 primary for physical safety content.

arc_updates.event: RF14-S-001 + RF14-S-002: car unsupervised + injury relay. ARC-RF14-001 signal count now 3. Pattern confirmed: Melissa deploying safety allegations against Eli's household. State remains WATCHING (promotion to ACTIVE requires 5+ confirmed signals or pattern escalation).
